title: Google sets Android Show: I/O Edition for May 12 as Gemini 4 preview looms
subtitle: Google has split its annual developer event in two — Android and on-device Gemini come first, then the full I/O keynote on May 19 with the new flagship model.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Google confirmed on May 11 that 'The Android Show: I/O Edition' will livestream May 12 at 10am PT, one week ahead of the main Google I/O 2026 keynote on May 19. The Android show will preview Android 17 and the on-device side of Gemini, with the full Gemini 4 model release expected at I/O proper. The split follows the structure Google used last year, separating consumer-facing OS news from the developer keynote.

The mechanism worth watching is agentic Android. Reports point to a project codenamed 'Remy' designed to let Gemini take actions across apps on a user's behalf — booking, replying, paying, navigating — without the user composing a separate prompt for each step. If that ships, it changes the unit of work on a phone from the app to the task.

Google is doing this against an unusual backdrop: Anthropic's Claude has captured most of the enterprise coding spend, OpenAI dominates consumer chat, and Chinese open-weights models are winning on cost. Gemini's strongest position is the one it already has — billions of Android devices, Workspace seats, and Search queries that competitors cannot directly substitute into. Whether Gemini 4 narrows the model-quality gap will matter, but the distribution moat may matter more.

For learners: the next year of AI on consumer devices will be defined less by 'which model is smartest' and more by which assistant can actually do things across the apps you already use. When you try out the new Gemini features, evaluate them by how many steps they remove from a real task you do, not by how impressive the demo looks.
